prerequisites: Account Administrator access, which is required to create, reveal, or manage API keys.
A Mailosaur API key, used as the `username` value for authentication.

authMethods.name: API key (HTTP Basic Auth)
authMethods.summary: Mailosaur authenticates over HTTP Basic Auth. Provide your Mailosaur API key as the basic auth username value (the connector's `username` field). No password is required, so the `password` field is optional and can be left empty. All requests must be made over HTTPS.

setupSteps.title: Create a Mailosaur API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Mailosaur Dashboard as an Account Administrator and open the API Keys section. Click 'Create standard key' for account-wide access, or 'Create server-restricted key' to limit the key to a single inbox. Enter a name, click Create, then use 'Reveal key' to copy the value. Keys do not expire.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key as the username
setupSteps.body: Set the required `username` field to your Mailosaur API key. Mailosaur uses HTTP Basic Auth with the API key supplied as the basic auth username.

setupSteps.title: Leave the password empty
setupSteps.body: The `password` field is optional. Mailosaur does not require a password for Basic Auth, so leave this field blank unless your environment needs it populated.

sources.title: Mailosaur Email and SMS Testing API reference (authentication)
